content.text:
I'll start by understanding the bug. The issue is that the oxc minifier removes an `if` guard condition, changing runtime behavior. The assignment inside the `if` block (`rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`) is being extracted outside the condition.

Let me first explore the repository structure and find relevant code.

content.text: This likely involves the minifier's handling of assignments and dead code. Let me look at the relevant

This is about if-statement minimization. The bug involves an assignment statement `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` inside an if block. The issue is likely in how the minifier handles if-statements that contain assignments. Let me look at the broader picture - particularly at how the minifier handles assignments to outer variables.

Let me first reproduce the bug:

input.description: Create test file with the bug repro
Run oxc minifier on test file

content.text:
I can reproduce the bug. The `if (rafId == null)` guard is removed and `rafId = ` assignment is also removed. The function body now just calls `requestAnimationFrame(...)` unconditionally.

Now let me understand what's happening. The minifier is likely treating the assignment as having no side effects on the outer variable and removing both the condition and the assignment itself. Let me dig into the compressor and the dead code removal.
